news.title: Karnataka housing board colony will soon get amenities
news.para: Residents of KHB Amargol Phase 2 Colony have secured a court order compelling the Karnataka Housing Board to pay Rs 8 crore to the Hubballi-Dharwad Municipal Corporation for essential amenities. This follows a petition highlighting infrastructure woes and a delay in the colony's handover.

news.title: Belagavi civic body collects ₹18 crore property tax in 20 days
news.para: Belagavi City Corporation has collected Rs 18.12 crore in property tax from April 1 to April 20. This strong start is due to a 5% early-payment rebate. Officials expect collections to rise further by the end of April. Digital payments are also seeing a significant increase. Discussions are ongoing about extending the rebate period.

news.title: Pay by May 31, avail property tax rebate: Chandigarh civic body urges residents
news.para: Chandigarh residents can avail property tax rebates by paying before May 31, with residential owners receiving 20% and commercial owners 10% off. Municipal Commissioner Amit Kumar urged timely payment to fund essential civic services like sanitation and road maintenance. Citizens are advised to check dues on the e-Sampark portal to ensure they receive these benefits.

news.title: Pimpri Chinchwad civic body seals 14 RMC plants for violation of pollution norms
news.para: Pimpri Chinchwad Municipal Corporation (PCMC) has sealed 14 Ready Mix Concrete (RMC) plants for serious pollution norm violations, including lack of dust suppression and improper material storage. This action follows a special drive and stricter guidelines issued by the Maharashtra Pollution Control Board (MPCB), with further enforcement planned.

news.title: Bihar cabinet approves satellite towns, tech park and ITI revamp
news.para: Under the leadership of CM Samrat Choudhary, Bihar's cabinet has taken a major step forward by approving the establishment of 11 newly named satellite townships, alongside a dynamic research park at IIT-Patna aimed at elevating the technological stature of eastern India.

news.title: Bengaluru: City corporations under GBA may purchase properties to recover unpaid taxes
news.para: With over 1.7 lakh defaulters and massive revenue losses, authorities are tightening enforcement, using surveys and a new acquisition strategy to push compliance and deter chronic tax evasion. After failed auctions and poor response, civic bodies plan direct property acquisition at guidance value to tackle mounting unpaid tax burden

news.title: FIR against four for constructions on Noida airport land
news.para: Authorities have filed an FIR against four individuals for constructing illegal structures on land designated for the Noida International Airport's expansion in Jewar. Despite a notification prohibiting new construction and transactions on this notified land, unauthorized building was discovered. This action aims to prevent inflated compensation claims under the Resettlement and Rehabilitation Scheme.

news.title: Over 6,500 houses covered in five-day anti-encroachment drive in Gurugram
news.para: A five-day anti-encroachment drive in Gurugram concluded, clearing public land and widening roads across prominent colonies. While many welcomed the move, others questioned the suddenness and uneven enforcement of demolitions, which included extended boundary walls, gates, and kiosks. The campaign is set to continue, with officials urging residents to self-remove violations.

news.title: Pimpri Chinchwad mayor seeks list of housing societies polluting rivers within seven days
news.para: Pimpri Chinchwad Mayor Ravi Landge has ordered strict action against housing societies releasing untreated sewage into local nullahs. The civic environment department must identify and penalize non-compliant societies within seven days. This directive follows complaints of pollution in the Pavana river. Officials found negligent will also face disciplinary action.

news.title: Singapore's Keppel posts slightly lower quarterly profit on weaker real estate
news.para: In its first-quarter financial update, Singapore's Keppel Ltd revealed a slight decline in profits, primarily due to reduced contributions from real estate ventures. On a brighter note, the company experienced a notable increase in asset management fees. Fortunately, Keppel's involvement in the region is minimal, shielding it from the ongoing Middle Eastern tensions.

news.title: Allahabad HC stays land acquisition proceedings for schemes of Uttar Pradesh's housing body
news.para: The Allahabad High Court has intervened in the land acquisition process for the Uttar Pradesh Awas Evam Vikas Parishad initiatives in Ayodhya, placing a hold on all relevant sites. Petitioners contend that the compensation offered under the 1965 Act pales in comparison to that available through the 2013 law.

news.title: Mumbai sessions court grants anticipatory bail to builder Sumit Jain in Lalbaug redevelopment 'fraud' case
news.para: Mumbai Sessions Court granted anticipatory bail to builder Sumit Jain in an alleged fraud case concerning the Lalbaug Redevelopment Project. The court cited a civil dispute angle, noting the project's delay due to inter-se disputes and a lack of satisfactory explanation for the FIR's delay.

news.title: NCLAT reserves order on Vedanta's pleas against selection of Adani's bid for JAL
news.para: The National Company Law Appellate Tribunal has reserved its decision on Vedanta's challenge. Vedanta questioned the selection of Adani Enterprises' bid for debt-ridden Jaiprakash Associates.  Adani Enterprises' bid was selected over Vedanta's higher offer. The Committee of Creditors defended their evaluation process. Jaiprakash Associates has significant assets across various sectors.

news.title: Havells India's net profit up 14.90% in FY26, lighting segment's profit up 32.10%
news.para: Havells India announced strong financial results for the fiscal year 2025-26. The company's net profit after tax saw a significant increase of 14.90 percent. Total income also grew by 4.26 percent. The board recommended a final dividend of ₹6 per equity share. The lighting and fixtures segment showed a nominal revenue growth.

news.title: Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis to lead power-packed Maharashtra Business Summit 2026 in Mumbai
news.para: In April 2026, Maharashtra is set to become the epicenter of innovation and business excellence with a groundbreaking summit and awards ceremony. Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis is invited as the chief guest, aiming to ignite discussions on propelling Maharashtra to the forefront as India’s first trillion-dollar sub-national economy.

news.title: Bengaluru metro expansion to drive 5-7 million sq ft office demand along Pink Line: Report
news.para: Office space demand of 5-7 million sq ft is expected along Bengaluru’s upcoming Pink Line and operational Yellow Line over the next two years, with improved metro connectivity set to boost real estate development and push residential prices up to 40% by 2027.

news.title: India real estate inflows hit $5.1 billion in Q1 2026: Report
news.para: Capital inflows into Indian real estate surged 72% year-on-year to a record $5.1 billion in Q1 2026, driven largely by domestic investors and REITs. Developers and REITs together accounted for over 80% of total investments, with major cities dominating capital deployment.

news.title: Spain approves a plan to ease its housing crisis
news.para: Spain's government has launched a major 7 billion euro plan to tackle its housing crisis. This initiative significantly boosts investment in public housing over the next four years. The plan aims to provide affordable homes for citizens, especially young people. It also focuses on renovating existing properties and improving energy efficiency.
